example_prompt: Capture an authorized (pre-authorized) payment transaction, fully or partially, to finalize the charge.

transactionId.description: UUID of the transaction in 'authorized' status to capture. Passed as a query parameter, not in the request body.

amount.description: Amount to capture. Must not exceed the originally authorized amount. Pass the full authorized amount for a full capture, or a smaller amount for a partial capture.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code matching the original authorization. Example: "XOF", "GNF".

response_schema.description: HTTP 202 — capture request accepted and queued for processing. No body returned. Monitor completion via webhook (status: SUCCEEDED or CAPTURED) or by calling verify_transaction.

details.description: Bictorys error code. Example: "E400-50: Amount exceed available capture".

gotchas: transactionId is a query parameter, not a path segment or body field. The URL must be: POST /pay/v1/captures?transactionId={uuid}. Sending transactionId in the body will be ignored.
The transaction must be in 'authorized' status (created with authorization: true in create_charge). Attempting to capture a 'succeeded' or 'failed' transaction returns an error.

This endpoint returns HTTP 202 (accepted), not 200. The capture is processed asynchronously. Monitor the final status via webhook or by calling verify_transaction and checking for status 'succeeded' or 'captured'.
The capture amount must not exceed the originally authorized amount — E400-50: Amount exceed available capture. For a full capture, pass the exact authorized amount.
Sandbox base URL is https://api.test.bictorys.com — the production URL is https://api.bictorys.com.
